title: Dilochia cantleyi
intro: Dilochia cantleyi, also known as the Himalayan climbing fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dilochia cantleyi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Dilochia cantleyi is often used to prepare traditional herbal remedies, such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in some modern herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Dilochia cantleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries like Indonesia and Malaysia. Dilochia cantleyi belongs to the genus Dilochia, which is part of the larger family Dipterocarpaceae. This family consists of over 700 species of trees and shrubs found in tropical regions. Dilochia cantleyi is often classified as an evergreen tree, adding to its unique characteristics.

Its leaves, roots, and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Traditional medicine practitioners use Dilochia cantleyi to treat skin conditions, like eczema and wounds.
